member.name: Charlotte Nichols

addresses: Office of Charlotte Nichols MP, Orford Jubilee Neighbourhood Hub, Jubilee Way, Warrington, WA2 8HE

member.synopsis: Charlotte Nichols is the Labour MP for Warrington North , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to ensure that investment in the civil nuclear sector supports the long-term resilience of the UK's wider nuclear skills base.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government’s commitment and investment in the civil nuclear sector provides greater certainty to industry, which supports recruitment and workforce development, Long-term resilience of the UK’s wider nuclear skills base is strengthened through delivery of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Skilled Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what discussions he has had with the Secretary of State for Defence on ensuring a skilled workforce for the (a) civil and (b) defence nuclear sectors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the Nuclear Skills Plan, the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero and Ministry of Defence work collaboratively with the nuclear industry organisations and academia in a coordinated effort to deliver the skilled workforce required for the ci...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Economic Growth and Job Creation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of large-scale nuclear in creating jobs and on economic growth.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Gigawatt-scale projects are delivering major benefits to local and national economies in support of the government’s growth agenda. EDF reports that the Hinkley Point C project supports 26,000 direct and indirect jobs across Britain and contributes £13.3 ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power: Supply Chains
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, in what way a fleet-deployment approach to gigawatt-scale nuclear development will support the civil nuclear supply chain and help ensure its long-term readiness and competitiveness.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Gigawatt-scale projects are delivering major benefits to local and national economies in support of the government’s growth agenda. EDF reports that the Hinkley Point C project supports 26,000 direct and indirect jobs across Britain and contributes £13.3 ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of current school building standards in protecting pupils and staff from extreme heat, and what steps her Department is taking to improve school infrastructure to en
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer my hon. Friend, the member for Warrington North to the answer of 30 June 2026 to Question 13129.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Processed Food: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make it his policy to mandate clear label markings on packaged food products to indicate excess a) salt, b) fat, c) sugar, d) trans and saturated fats and e) calories.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Food labelling legislation requires that pre-packaged food and drinks include a mandatory nutrition declaration, which provides information on energy, in kilojoules and kilocalories, and key nutrients, including fat, saturated fat, sugar and salt, in gram...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Processed Food: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make it her policy to mandate clear labelling of ultra-processed food.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Scientific Advisory Committee on Nutrition (SACN) has considered the impact of processed foods and health in 2023 and 2025. The limitations in the available evidence on processed and ultra-processed foods and health means it is unclear whether these f...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: Training and Work Experience
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps the Department is taking to help improve employer-led training and workplace experience within further and higher education, particularly in construction; and what assessment he has made of h
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 23 March 2025, the Chancellor announced a £625 million Construction Skills Package (CSP) to address critical skills shortages across the construction sector. The investment is expected to support up to 60,000 skilled workers over this Parliament by inc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: BBC: Music
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what discussions she has had with the BBC on the potential impact on the its ability to host live music events, like the BBC Radio 6 Music Festival, around the UK following the Corporation’s anno
writtenQuestions.answerText: The BBC is a vital national institution and an engine of the nations and regions. In that context, this news is concerning for the BBC workforce who are facing significant uncertainty, and for the sector as a whole. The BBC is delivering significant savin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: BBC: Local Broadcasting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, how her Department plans to help ensure that the BBC can continue to a) invest in regional storytelling; and b) support growth in regional creative economies under a new Charter.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a public service broadcaster that matters hugely to public life, the BBC must be responsive to its viewers and listeners, in order to tell inclusive stories about the lives of all people, in all parts of the UK. This Government wants broadcasters, incl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate his Department has made of the increased health costs caused by AI displacement in the workforce and associated stresses; and if he will make a statement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Drugs: Research

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government responded to ACMD recommendations on reducing barriers to clinical research with Schedule 1 drugs in July 2025. A cross-government officials group is in place and is developing the policy and has engaged with other departments, agencies, st...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government responded to ACMD recommendations on reducing barriers to clinical research with Schedule 1 drugs in July 2025. A cross-government officials group is in place and is developing the policy and has engaged with other departments, agencies, st...

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Energy Supply
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his Department has made of the potential level of contribution that new large-scale nuclear power stations could make to enhancing the UK's energy security.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Large-scale nuclear power stations, including Hinkley Point C and Sizewell C, will continue to make a vital contribution to our homegrown clean and secure energy mix, complementing small modular reactors. To pursue the option of further potential large-sc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what plans his Department has to support additional large-scale nuclear power stations beyond Hinkley Point C and Sizewell C.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To pursue the option of further potential large-scale nuclear beyond Hinkley Point C and Sizewell C, the government has tasked Great British Energy - Nuclear (GBE-N) with identifying other suitable sites that could potentially host such a project. GBE-N w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Closures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the scheduled closure of Torness, Hartlepool and Heysham nuclear power stations on baseload electricity supply.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Currently, nuclear generation provides around 15% of total electricity. The four Advanced Gas-cooled Reactor (AGR) stations at these locations are operating beyond their original forecasts, with current expected closures being planned well in advance by t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Nuclear Power Stations: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his Department has made of the suitability of sites across the United Kingdom for new large-scale nuclear power station development.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To pursue the option of further potential large-scale nuclear beyond Hinkley Point C and Sizewell C, the government has tasked Great British Energy - Nuclear (GBE-N) with identifying other suitable sites that could potentially host such a project.GBE-N wi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Environment Agency: Local Government

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Environment Agency’s Report an environmental problem service enables members of the public to report environmental issues in a more modern and accessible way. The service can be used for people to report water pollution, smells from waste facilities, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Data Protection: Productivity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the answer of 20 March 2026 to question 120026, if she will make it her policy to make a definitive empirical study specifying UK-wide GDPR impact on productivity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government keeps the impacts of data protection legislation under review. As set out in the answer of 20 March 2026 to Question 120026, there is currently no definitive empirical study that isolates the specific, UK‑wide impact of the UK GDPR on produ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Juries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, pursuant to his answer of 18 March to question 120027, whether he will publish the results of his department's engagement with the judiciary about their personal safety and security associated with the Courts and
writtenQuestions.answerText: We engage regularly with judicial leaders on all matters including security. It is standard practice not to publish or comment on the specifics of discussions between Ministers and the judiciary.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

items.text: Name of donor: Ratch Group PLC Address of donor: 72 Ngamwongwan Road Bang Khen, Mueang Nonthaburi, Nonthaburi 11000 Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£4,197.39), accommodation (£523.70), value £4,721.09 Destination of visit: Thailand (Bangkok) Dates of visit: 14 July 2025 to 18 July 2025 Purpose of visit: Attending "A Global Dialogue on Small Modular Reactor Deployment" conference at the Queen Sikirit National Convention Centre in Bangkok, on 16 July 2025, which I was invited to attend and speak at in my capacity as Chair of the Nuclear APPG. (Registered 6 August 2025)
